strengths: Thorough cross-cloud detection: SF, MuleSoft, MC, Health Cloud all identified
API-led layer decomposition clearly mapped across 3 sibling stories
25 sibling stories under parent feature analyzed for dependency context
DevInt2 metadata grounded the consent object model assessment
15 derived ACs cover happy path, error handling, security, and performance
All 10 mandatory lenses populated with specific, non-generic content
10 anti-pattern gates evaluated — MuleSoft layering and consent SOT explicitly justified
Cross-cloud implications identified for MuleSoft, MC, and Data Cloud future
weaknesses: No explicit AC for MC → SF double opt-in confirmation callback (return path)
Effort estimate (21 story points) may underestimate MC API integration complexity

findings.message: Missing AC for double opt-in confirmation return path from MC to SF
findings.recommendation: Add AC-16: MC sends confirmation callback to MuleSoft sys-api → prc-api → exp-api → SF platform event to update ConsentStatus to 'Opt In'

strengths: 4 ADRs with clear context, decision, consequences, and rejected alternatives
Expert Context block generated with platform fit, tradeoffs, anti-patterns, re-architecture triggers
CTA domain coverage matrix shows 8/9 domains addressed
RAML contract specification with DataWeave transformation map
Sequence diagrams cover happy path, error/DLQ path, and circuit breaker scenarios
weaknesses: Security architecture references mTLS but does not specify certificate management approach
Scale analysis identifies MC API rate limit as bottleneck but doesn't quantify current consent volume

findings.message: mTLS certificate management approach not specified
findings.recommendation: Add certificate lifecycle management using Anypoint Platform trust store or external PKI

findings.message: Consent volume estimate needed for capacity planning
findings.recommendation: Query DevInt2 for ContactPointTypeConsent record count over last 30 days to baseline volume

strengths: 28 test cases covering all 15 ACs with clear Given/When/Then structure
Test case naming convention follows TC_Output_RTM format
DataWeave transformation tests include edge cases (null phone, international format)
RTM maps every AC to at least 2 test cases
Test data factory pattern with configurable consent states
weaknesses: No chaos/fault injection test for MuleSoft runtime failures

strengths: 12 findings properly categorized with actionable remediation
Anti-pattern analysis evaluated all 10 gates
Cross-cloud contract coupling identified as key risk
RAML spec, DataWeave modules, and MuleSoft flow XML documented
All 12 review findings addressed with resolution steps
weaknesses: Implementation is pseudo-code — cannot verify compilation or unit test execution
Anypoint Platform credentials not available for CloudHub deployment

findings.message: Full build requires Anypoint Studio with CloudHub credentials
findings.recommendation: Provision Anypoint Platform sandbox for development team; run mvn clean test locally

strengths: 14 E2E scenarios well-structured with clear entry/exit criteria
RTM maps all ACs to test cases with honest status reporting (47% BLOCKED)
Blocker chain correctly identifies NGASIM-3058 as the critical path dependency
weaknesses: Zero tests executed — all blocked by upstream dependencies
MC sandbox not provisioned — cannot validate MC API integration even with mocks

findings.message: No E2E test execution possible — 4 blockers (NGASIM-3058, -3335, -3143, MC sandbox)
findings.recommendation: Track blockers in sprint board; target E2E execution in Sprint N+1 after NGASIM-3058 reaches Done

strengths: Comprehensive STRIDE threat model with 16 identified threats
OWASP API Security Top 10 fully mapped to MuleSoft context
PII/PHI analysis correctly identifies HIPAA-adjacent classification for medical device consent data
Cross-cloud trust boundary analysis covers SF → MuleSoft → MC boundaries
Consent compliance section covers TCPA, CAN-SPAM, GDPR
weaknesses: 2 CRITICAL findings remain unmitigated: PII in logs, credentials in properties
mTLS not configured — recommended but not enforced
No penetration test planned for MuleSoft exp-api endpoints

findings.message: PII (phone numbers) logged in clear text in MuleSoft flow logs and DLQ messages
findings.recommendation: Implement DataWeave PII masker for all log statements; encrypt DLQ message payloads

findings.message: MC API OAuth2 credentials stored as application properties instead of secure vault
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Anypoint Secure Properties or HashiCorp Vault before any non-sandbox deployment

findings.message: Consent state machine not enforced — out-of-order events could corrupt consent status
findings.recommendation: Add state transition validation in DataWeave before routing to prc-api

strengths: Design quality rated 7.4/10 with justified scoring
All 10 anti-pattern gates evaluated with evidence
Reusability analysis identifies 4 future consent types that can reuse exp-api pattern
Operational runbook covers deployment, monitoring, 7 failure modes, rollback
Traceability map chains stories → ACs → ADRs → tests → findings → debt
Cross-cloud integration guide with credential rotation procedures
CTA 9-domain coverage summary with recommended follow-ups

conditions: Resolve 2 CRITICAL security findings (PII logging, credential storage) before any non-sandbox deployment
Complete NGASIM-3058 → enable integration testing → re-run E2E stage
Validate MC Classic REST API approach with MC admin team per ADR-004
Add AC-16 for double opt-in confirmation return path
Schedule security penetration test for MuleSoft exp-api before production
expertGaps: MC sandbox not provisioned for validation
Consent volume baseline not established from DevInt2 data
